req.title: Fraud alert response
req.statement: A customer shall confirm or deny a suspected-fraud alert within 24 hours; unacknowledged alerts shall auto-freeze the account.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-013 defines a state machine with three key branches: (1) customer confirms fraud within 24 h → alert closed + card reissued, (2) customer denies fraud within 24 h → alert closed, no reissue, (3) alert older than 24 h → account frozen + 410 on attempted response. Test design covers EP (valid confirm/deny partition), STC (alert active → closed transition), DT (confirm vs deny decision), NEG (expired alert 410 error), and BVA (response submitted at exactly 24 h boundary). The violation in the original test[4] was kind=negative paired with heuristic=EP, which is logically inconsistent — EP partitions represent valid equivalence classes and belong to positive tests. Fix: change kind to 'positive' for the auto-freeze account status test since it verifies expected system behaviour (the frozen state) within a valid partition of the 'no response' equivalence class. Each test is idempotent via fixture-managed alert IDs that are created fresh per test.

tests.name: test_confirm_fraud_alert_closes_alert_and_reissues_card
preconditions: A fraud alert is active for the customer account
Alert age is less than 24 hours
Customer is authenticated with a valid token
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body field card_reissued equals true
trace: R-FIN-013: alert closed when customer confirms
R-FIN-013: card reissued if fraud confirmed
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_confirm_fraud_alert_closes_alert_and_reissues_card(
    active_alert_fixture, auth_headers
):
    alert_id = active_alert_fixture(age_hours=1, response_type="confirm")
    resp = <api_client>.patch(
        f"/alerts/{alert_id}",
        json={"response": "confirm"},
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["alert_status"] == "closed"
    assert body["card_reissued"] is True

tests.name: test_deny_fraud_alert_closes_alert_without_card_reissue
preconditions: A fraud alert is active for the customer account
Alert age is less than 24 hours
Customer is authenticated with a valid token
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body field card_reissued equals false
trace: R-FIN-013: alert closed when customer denies fraud
R-FIN-013: card is NOT reissued when fraud denied
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_deny_fraud_alert_closes_alert_without_card_reissue(
    active_alert_fixture, auth_headers
):
    alert_id = active_alert_fixture(age_hours=1, response_type="deny")
    resp = <api_client>.patch(
        f"/alerts/{alert_id}",
        json={"response": "deny"},
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["alert_status"] == "closed"
    assert body["card_reissued"] is False

tests.name: test_expired_alert_returns_410
preconditions: A fraud alert exists but was created more than 24 hours ago
Account has been auto-frozen due to no acknowledgement
Customer is authenticated with a valid token
actions: PATCH /alerts/{id} with body {"response": "confirm"} on an expired alert
Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: R-FIN-013: error pathway 410 when alert expired
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_expired_alert_returns_410(expired_alert_fixture, auth_headers):
    alert_id = expired_alert_fixture(age_hours=25)
    resp = <api_client>.patch(
        f"/alerts/{alert_id}",
        json={"response": "confirm"},
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 410
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error_code") == "ALERT_EXPIRED"
    account_resp = <api_client>.get(
        "/accounts/me", headers=auth_headers
    )
    assert account_resp.json()["status"] == "frozen"

tests.name: test_response_at_exactly_24h_boundary_accepted
preconditions: A fraud alert is active and was created exactly 24 hours ago (boundary condition)
Customer is authenticated with a valid token
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: HTTP 200 returned (alert still within window at boundary)
No 410 error returned at the exact boundary
trace: R-FIN-013: customer shall confirm within 24 hours — boundary at exactly 24 h
BVA on the 24-hour time window upper boundary

tests.name: test_unacknowledged_alert_auto_freezes_account
preconditions: A fraud alert was active but 24 hours have elapsed with no response
Auto-freeze job has executed (simulated via test fixture)
Customer is authenticated with a valid token
actions: GET /accounts/me to retrieve account status after alert expiry
Capture HTTP response status and account status field
trace: R-FIN-013: unacknowledged alerts shall auto-freeze the account
EP: partition where customer never responds — expected system outcome is frozen account
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_unacknowledged_alert_auto_freezes_account(
    expired_alert_fixture, auth_headers, trigger_auto_freeze
):
    expired_alert_fixture(age_hours=25)
    trigger_auto_freeze()
    resp = <api_client>.get("/accounts/me", headers=auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "frozen"
    assert body.get("card_reissued") is not True
